LAW AND MOTION 302, "IN RESPONSE TO THE COVID-19 OUTBREAK, THE HEARING DATES FOR ALL LAW AND MOTION AND DISCOVERY MATTERS CURRENTLY CALENDARED IN DEPARTMENTS 301/302 ARE HEREBY VACATED. WHEN THE COURT RESUMES NORMAL OPERATIONS, THE PRESIDING JUDGE WILL ANNOUNCE PROCEDURES FOR SETTING HEARING DATES FOR THOSE CASES WHICH HAVE BEEN FULLY BRIEFED. OPPOSITIONS WILL BE DUE BASED ON THE RESCHEDULED HEARING DATE, BUT NOTHING PRECLUDES A PARTY FROM FILING AN OPPOSITION EARLIER." (302)
MINI MINUTES FOR JUL-31-2020 09:31 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS PAGANI WORLDWIDE LLC, FRANCESCO ZAPPACOSTA, PAGANI AUTOMOBILE AMERICA, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT: THE HEARING ON DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER IS CONTINUED TO AUGUST 28, 2020 TO PERMIT PLAINTIFF JAMES HILTON JR. TO FILE A COMPLIANT OPPOSITION. AS DEFENDANTS NOTE, THE LIMIT FOR SUCH AN OPPOSITION IS 15 PAGES. (CRC 3.1113(D).) MR. HILTON CORRECTLY STATES THAT A DEMURRER TESTS ONLY THE LEGAL SUFFICIENCY OF THE COMPLAINT'S ALLEGATIONS, SO HE MAY WISH TO RECONSIDER HIS OPPOSITION'S LENGTHY "STATEMENT OF FACTS." MR. HILTON'S 15-PAGE OPPOSITION IS DUE ON AUGUST 14, 2020; ANY FURTHER REPLY IS DUE AUGUST 21, 2020. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/RBU)
ORDER SUSTAINING DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PAGANI WORLDWIDE LLC, FRANCESCO ZAPPACOSTA, PAGANI AUTOMOBILE AMERICA, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT PAGANI'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD FACTS SHOWING A TIMELY CLAIM AND EXHAUSTION OF ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES. ZAPPACOSTA'S DEMURRER TO CAUSES OF ACTION ONE AND THREE ARE SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF CAN ONLY PLEAD THOSE DISCHARGE CLAIMS AGAINST HIS EMPLOYER; THEY ARE NOT COGNIZABLE AGAINST HIS MANAGER. (SEE JONES V. TORREY PINES PARTNERSHIP (2008) 42 CAL.4TH 1158, 1173; RENO V. BAIRD (1998) 18 CAL.4TH 640, 662.) ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-29-2020 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-02-2020 Continued To Nov-18-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendant Pagani Worldwide Llc, Francesco Zappacosta, Pagani Automobile America, Inc. Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint (Stipulated Continuance)
Off Calendar Aug-28-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Pagani Worldwide Llc, Francesco Zappacosta, Pagani Automobile America, Inc.'s Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint.

Defendant Pagani's demurrer to the first amended complaint is sustained with leave to amend to plead facts showing a timely claim and exhaustion of administrative remedies. Zappacosta's demurrer to causes of action one and three are sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff can only plead those discharge claims against his employer; they are not cognizable against his manager. (See Jones v. Torrey Pines Partnership (2008) 42 Cal.4th 1158, 1173; Reno v. Baird (1998) 18 Cal.4th 640, 662.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-16-2020 Continued To Sep-16-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendant Pagani Worldwide Llc, Francesco Zappacosta, Pagani Automobile America, Inc. Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint (Stipulated Continuance)
Off Calendar Jul-31-2020 Continued To Aug-28-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendants Pagani Worldwide LLC, Francesco Zappacosta, Pagani Automobile America, Inc.'s Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint:

The hearing on defendants' demurrer is continued to August 28, 2020 to permit plaintiff James Hilton Jr. to file a compliant opposition. As defendants note, the limit for such an opposition is 15 pages. (CRC 3.1113(d).) Mr. Hilton correctly states that a demurrer tests only the legal sufficiency of the complaint's allegations, so he may wish to reconsider his opposition's lengthy "statement of facts." Mr. Hilton's 15-page opposition is due on August 14, 2020; any further reply is due August 21, 2020.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
